text: Bad morning. Could not get the Cato chapter to land. The index cards are strong but the structure is not there yet. Took a walk to the state park instead of forcing it.

Rough night, maybe five hours of sleep. Still got the walk in with the boys; Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing this morning felt like pushing through mud, specifically couldn't get the Cato section to move.

2026-05-19 morning notes: Slept roughly five hours (fitful). Still managed 6:00 a.m. walk with Liam and Theo; highlight: Liam discovered a turtle near the mailbox — boys fascinated, paused to watch it retract and re-emerge. Writing block afterwards felt heavy, particularly the Cato subsection; metaphorical mud. No clear structural breakthrough.

Morning entry: ~5 hours fragmented sleep. Kept morning routine: 6:00 walk with Liam & Theo; highlight was Liam spotting a turtle by the mailbox – boys observed its movements. Writing session afterwards (Cato section) felt like pushing through mud—structure unclear and no progress. Emotional tone: tired but steady.

Finished a reread of Seneca's Letters from a Stoic on today's run in Bastrop State Park. Noted that Seneca on wasted time hit differently this read — connected it to what he's working through with the Cato chapter of Discipline Is Destiny.

Finished rereading Letters from a Stoic during today's run. Seneca's passage on wasted time struck me harder than previous reads, especially given the ongoing struggle with the Cato chapter structure. Noted a sense that I'm letting the draft ramble—too many anecdotes, not enough argument. The line about "life is long if you know how to use it" felt like a direct admonishment.

Rough night — maybe five hours of sleep. Still got the sunrise walk in with Liam and Theo; highlight was Liam spotting a small box turtle right by the mailbox. Logged it carefully and let the boys watch it crawl off into the grass.

Sat down for the Cato section after journaling but the pages felt like moving through mud. Nothing flowed. The argument still isn't landing. Low energy, low focus. Seneca's line about "when you are tired, reason is tired too" kept floating up.

Need to sort out sleep tonight or tomorrow will be the same grind.

Finished a reread of Seneca's Letters from a Stoic during today's run. The passages on wasted time landed harder than they have in the past—feels like a mirror held up to the stalled Cato section. Noticing how fatigue blurs the edges of discipline.

Rough night — maybe five hours. Did the walk with the boys anyway. Liam found a turtle near the mailbox, which is the kind of thing that makes the walk worth doing regardless of everything else. Writing this morning was slow and resistant. The Cato section isn't yielding. Second day in a row of this particular combination: short sleep, stuck chapter, mud-feeling. The pattern is consistent — under six hours and the cards don't want to move.
